LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ' MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEESB IS GRANTED. HAVING SUED DEFENDANT UNDER A WRITTEN CONTRACT WITH A FEES CLAUSE, PLAINTIFF IS SUBJECT TO THE FEES CLAUSE NOTWITHSTANDING DEFENDANT HAVING SUCCESSFULLY ARGUED THAT HE WAS NOT BOUND BY THE CONTRACT. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT THE PARTIES DID NOT HAVE A WRITTEN CONTRACT AND THE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OF CCP 339 APPLIES TO THIS CASE. THE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ALSO APPLIES TO THE ACCOUNT STATED CLAIM UNDER IVERSON, YOAKUM, PAPIANO & HATCH V. BERWALD (1999) 76 CAL.APP.4TH 990, 995-997. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE TIME BARRED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ'S MOTION FOR AMENDMENT OF JUDGMENT FOLLOWING AWARDS OF ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED. THE JUDGMENT WILL BE AMENDED TO INCLUDE $20,440.36 IN FEES AND COSTS, PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S NOVEMBER 18, 2013 AND SEPTEMBER 16, 2014 ORDERS. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION DOES NOT SET FORTH A BASIS TO DENY THIS MOTION. PLAINTIFF MAKES ARGUMENTS CONCERNING THE UNDERLYING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND FEE MOTIONS; HOWEVER, THE TIME TO SEEK RECONSIDERATION OF THESE ORDERS HAS EXPIRED. (SEE CCP SEC. 1008.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. AMENDED JUDGMENT TO BE SUBMITTED AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Amendment Of Judgment Following Awards Of Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES MILLAR OF MILLAR & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 981-8100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ'S Motion For Amendment Of Judgment Following Awards Of Attorney Fees And Costs is Granted. The Judgment will be amended to include $20,440.36 in fees and costs, pursuant to the Court's November 18, 2013 and September 16, 2014 Orders. Plaintiff's opposition does not set forth a basis to deny this motion. Plaintiff makes arguments concerning the underlying motion for summary judgment and fee motions; however, the time to seek reconsideration of these Orders has expired. (See CCP sec. 1008.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. AMENDED JUDGMENT TO BE SUBMITTED AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ's Motion For Award Of Attorney Fees Upon Appellate Remittitur is Granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $3,060.00 within 30 days of entry of this Order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

JAMES MILLAR, ESQ., 415.981.8100, FOR DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ' motion For Award Of Attorneys FeesB IS Granted. Having sued defendant under a written contract with a fees clause, plaintiff is subject to the fees clause notwithstanding defendant having successfully argued that he was not bound by the contract. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that the parties did not have a written contract and the two year statute of limitations of CCP 339 applies to this case. The two year statute of limitations also applies to the account stated claim under Iverson, Yoakum, Papiano & Hatch v. Berwald (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 990, 995-997. Plaintiff's claims are time barred. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
